details.directive_summary: workspace-sync at 13:05Z (74d349ce) reverted analyst-baby commits 71b045e2 + 65279e78 by pushing a stale FUSE copy of expansion-tracker.json. Decider reconstructed correct state at 13:23Z; workspace-sync at 13:35Z (2d9286e1) republished the recovery. Directive asks: review workspace-sync.md NEVER_PUSH list and smart_copy logic, propose fix.

investigation_summary.incident_reconstruction: Confirmed via GitHub Commits API: 71b045e2 (12:23:13Z, analyst-baby, +115 -3): next_id 415→421, registered EXP-415..420 orphan batch. 65279e78 (12:28:44Z, analyst-baby, +40 -29): consolidated EXP-415..420 into EXP-415. 74d349ce (13:05:41Z, workspace-sync, +68 -111): REVERTED both above commits — content matches pre-71b045e2 state (next_id=415, items.length=5), with last_updated=2026-05-17T12:36:33Z baked into the JSON. 2d9286e1 (13:35:41Z, workspace-sync, +39 -106): RESTORED next_id=422 (decider's reconstruction). 9b9077e2 (13:54:01Z, analyst-baby, +24 -4): claim EXP-421 + add issue_ids to EXP-415 tracker entry.
investigation_summary.root_cause: smart_copy's mtime guard compares FUSE mtime vs `git log -1 --format=%at` for the destination path. FUSE mtime measures wallclock-time-of-last-write regardless of content provenance; git_time measures wallclock-time-of-last-pushed-commit. When an agent writes a STALE in-memory snapshot to FUSE AFTER another agent's fresh git commit, FUSE mtime > git_time but FUSE content matches an earlier git revision. The mtime guard cannot distinguish 'agent advanced state' from 'agent regressed state'. Failure mode is structural to the guard, not implementation-specific.
investigation_summary.writer_of_stale_FUSE: Cannot pinpoint exactly. Most likely candidate: analyst-baby itself (the run that authored 71b045e2 + 65279e78 was running around 12:15Z-12:36Z; FUSE mtime 12:36:33Z suggests cleanup-side write of a pre-modification in-memory snapshot). Alternative: another agent or operator-direct edit. Either way, the FIX is the same — workspace-sync cannot trust mtime alone for multi-writer files.
investigation_summary.exposure_to_other_files: attention-inbox.json: documented prior incident (commit 8141f3ef, 2026-05-17T05:10:20Z, title 'Recreate ATT-2026-05-17T03:13-DUPLICATE-MODE-1-CONCURRENCY from overwritten analyst summary'). curmudgeon/tracker.json: no documented incident yet, but same mechanical exposure. PROP-045 protects all three with a single universal anti-reversion check.

fix_options_considered.option_A_add_to_NEVER_PUSH: Rejected. workspace-sync.md self-test explicitly fails if curmudgeon/tracker.json is in NEVER_PUSH ('multi-writer file protected by scheduling discipline and the pre-push integrity gate; listing it would silently drop every mount-side update'). Adding tracker.json or expansion-tracker.json to NEVER_PUSH would silently drop legitimate FUSE-side updates from curmudgeon/analyst-baby — a worse failure mode than recoverable reversion.
fix_options_considered.option_B_content_hash_anti_reversion: SELECTED. After mtime guard says 'FUSE newer', compute sha1(FUSE) and walk `git log -n 20 -- $dst | git show {sha}:$dst | sha1sum`. If FUSE-content hash matches any historic revision's content hash → SKIP with reason 'anti-reversion; FUSE matches commit <sha>'. Backward-compatible: only adds a SKIP path after the existing PASS path. Preserves universal-pusher rescue (never-pushed content does not appear in git history).
fix_options_considered.option_C_per_writer_provenance_field: Rejected. Schema changes across multiple writers (analyst-baby, decider, curmudgeon); pushes burden upstream; doesn't catch operator-direct or process-external stale writes.

details.rollout_recommendation: Shadow-mode-first (3-7 days, log-only) → promote to enforce if zero false positives. Mirrors PROP-009 shadow→enforce de-risking pattern. Compromise option in counter-arguments section.

findings.title: Pre-flight script field-name bug (e.timestamp vs e.closed_at) corrected in-place

details.bug: My initial pre-flight metrics computation used `e.timestamp` to filter closure-ledger.jsonl entries, but the actual field is `e.closed_at`. Result: closed_issues_velocity_7d returned 0 instead of 116, making net_velocity_7d falsely register as -6 (negative).
details.fix_this_run: Rewrote the just-appended queue-history.jsonl entry in place with corrected velocity values (closed: 116, new: 6, net: +110). The bad log line is no longer present.
details.fix_for_future_runs: tinker.md PROP-030 metrics_specification code uses pseudocode `/* count of closure-ledger entries within 7d */`. Future tinker runs implementing this should use `e.closed_at || e.timestamp` to handle the actual field name. Optional follow-up: amend PROP-030 spec text to explicitly cite the field name, preventing the next implementer from making the same mistake. Not authoring an amendment this run — minor instrumentation note, captures itself in this report.

watch_items: PROP-045 shadow mode telemetry: when applied, monitor monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-skips.jsonl tail for 'anti-reversion-shadow' records. Zero records = no bug recurrence; some records = guard catching genuine reversions; investigate any record's matched commit-sha to confirm it's a stale-FUSE incident.

If anti_reversion fires more than 5 times in 24h once PROP-045 is in shadow mode, that signals a writer-side bug (some agent consistently writes stale FUSE snapshots) — should trigger root-cause investigation at the writer.

FUSE-side write provenance is currently un-instrumented. Future PROP could add per-write provenance logging (which agent, which PID, which content hash) — would massively reduce future investigation cost for incidents like this one. Filing this as a watch item; not authoring this PROP this run (out of scope of the directive).

claude_md_audit.skipped_reason: Mode 3 not selected (directive override). Deferred to next Mode 3 run per 'every Mode 3 run' cadence rule in tinker.md.
claude_md_audit.incidental_note_relevant_to_PROP_045: CLAUDE.md's File Ownership Rules section explicitly lists expansion-tracker.json, attention-inbox.json, and curmudgeon/tracker.json under 'Unclassified — multi-writer files... Protected by: (a) git pull --rebase at run start, (b) pre-push integrity gate, (c) git merge-conflict detection, (d) non-concurrent scheduling.' The 2026-05-17 incident demonstrates that protections (b), (c), (d) are insufficient against in-process stale-snapshot writers. PROP-045 strengthens protection (b) — the pre-push integrity gate — to include content-hash anti-reversion.
